response.justification: The role mandates 5–10 years of hands-on C++ development on Windows plus strong SQL Server/Oracle experience and a capital-markets or fixed-income domain background. The candidate's C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a university thesis, with a career otherwise built on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. There is no SQL Server/Oracle, no Windows-platform C++ focus, and no fixed-income or investment-banking systems experience (the RIDE Capital role was a TypeScript/GraphQL fintech product, not capital-markets engineering). The core mandatory requirements are unmet.
strengths: Broad, deep full-stack engineering experience across web, mobile, and embedded systems
Strong software-craftsmanship discipline (TDD, pairing, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
Some fintech-adjacent exposure at RIDE Capital and demonstrated low-latency, high-throughput systems work
concerns: No substantial C++ experience — only ~6 months professionally, far below the 5–10 year requirement, and none recent or Windows-based
No SQL Server/Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB/MySQL
No fixed-income, derivatives, or genuine capital-markets/investment-banking systems domain experience
